items.title: Potemkin Loader & RMMProject The Anatomy of a ClickFix Attack
items.description: A ClickFix social engineering attack on an unmonitored endpoint led to a multi-stage intrusion affecting over 11 hosts. The infection chain began with a malicious HTA payload that silently installed an MSI package containing Potemkin, a custom loader with a deterministic DGA. Potemkin delivered RMMProject, a 4.4 MB Lua-scriptable RAT featuring browser credential theft with Chrome App-Bound Encrypt
items.ai_summary: The ClickFix social engineering attack exploited an unmonitored endpoint, leading to a multi-stage intrusion that affected over 11 hosts. The infection chain began with a malicious HTA payload that silently installed an MSI package containing Potemkin, a custom loader with a deterministic Domain Generation Algorithm (DGA). This delivered RMMProject, a 4.4 MB Lua-scriptable Remote Access Trojan (RAT) that featured browser credential theft with Chrome App-Bound Encryption. To remediate, defenders should immediately block all HTA files at the email gateway and web proxy levels, and implement a software restriction policy to prevent the execution of unauthorized MSI packages. Additionally, defenders should update their Chrome browser to the latest version and ensure that all Chrome extensions are verified and up-to-date.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1204| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1104| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1219| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1555.003| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Attackers Weaponize Microsoft Teams Relays to Stay Hidden
items.description: Attackers deploying DragonForce ransomware against a major U.S. services firm concealed their command-and-control traffic within Microsoft Teams relay infrastructure using Backdoor.Turn, a custom Go-based remote access trojan. This novel technique leverages anonymous Teams visitor tokens and TURN relay servers to mask malicious communications as legitimate Microsoft traffic. The intrusion lasted o
items.ai_summary: The attackers exploited the Microsoft Teams relay infrastructure to conceal their command-and-control traffic, utilizing a custom Go-based remote access trojan known as Backdoor.Turn. This technique leveraged anonymous Teams visitor tokens and TURN relay servers to mask malicious communications as legitimate Microsoft traffic, allowing the attackers to remain hidden. The DragonForce ransomware was deployed against a major U.S. services firm, resulting in a significant intrusion. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement strict traffic monitoring and filtering, specifically targeting unusual patterns of Microsoft Teams traffic, and ensure that all remote access software is up-to-date and configured to use secure protocols. Additionally, defenders should consider implementing a security information and event management (SIEM) system to detect and respond to similar threats in the future.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Exploit Public-Facing Application]:::tactic
    B -->|T1071| C[Application Layer Protocol]:::tactic
    C -->|T1219| D[Remote Access Software]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Data Encrypted for Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Bluekit Phishing as a Service (PhaaS)
items.description: BlueKit operates as a mature commercial Phishing-as-a-Service platform offering 87 ready-made phishing kits targeting banks, cloud services, cryptocurrency exchanges, and global brands. The platform features subscription-based access, automated account takeover capabilities, peer-to-peer infrastructure for stealth, and integrated anti-detection tooling. BlueKit supports credential harvesting, sess
items.ai_summary: The BlueKit Phishing-as-a-Service (PhaaS) platform poses a significant threat to various organizations, including banks, cloud services, cryptocurrency exchanges, and global brands, by offering 87 ready-made phishing kits. These kits enable threat actors to compromise accounts through phishing attacks, leveraging tactics such as T1586 (Compromise Accounts) and T1078 (Valid Accounts), with the initial access often gained through T1566 (Phishing). If exploited, this can lead to unauthorized access to sensitive information, financial loss, and reputational damage, affecting both individuals and businesses. To remediate, defenders should implement robust email filtering solutions, conduct regular security awareness training for employees, and enforce multi-factor authentication (MFA) for all users, particularly those with privileged access. Additionally, monitoring for suspicious account activity and implementing a robust incident response plan can help mitigate the impact of such attacks.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1566| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1078| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1586| D[Impact]:::tactic
    D -->|T1078| E[Persistence]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: New APT-Q-27 sample spotted
items.description: A new campaign has been identified utilizing a valid digital signature from a Chinese technology company that remains unrevoked. The attack chain employs a dropper that retrieves an extension-based module list from command and control infrastructure. The malicious payloads exploit DLL Side-Loading techniques through a legitimate Tencent-signed executable to achieve code execution. The infrastructu
items.ai_summary: The newly identified APT-Q-27 sample utilizes a valid digital signature from a Chinese technology company to evade detection, employing a dropper that communicates with command and control infrastructure to retrieve an extension-based module list. This campaign exploits DLL Side-Loading techniques through a legitimate Tencent-signed executable, achieving code execution and potentially leading to unauthorized access and data breaches. The attack chain involves the use of Command and Scripting Interpreter (T1059) and Application Layer Protocol (T1071) tactics, highlighting the need for defenders to monitor network traffic and system calls closely. To remediate, defenders should implement strict validation of digital signatures, monitor for unusual executable behavior, and ensure that all software is updated to the latest versions, particularly focusing on Tencent and other potentially vulnerable applications. Specific remediation steps include configuring endpoint detection and response tools to flag DLL Side-Loading attempts and regularly reviewing command and control infrastructure communications to identify potential security breaches.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1059| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1574.002| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1071| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1041| E[Command and Control]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Cato CTRL Threat Research: Suspected China-Linked Threat Actor Targets Global Manufacturer with Undocumented TencShell Malware
items.description: In April 2026, Cato CTRL identified and blocked an attempted intrusion against a global manufacturing customer involving TencShell, a previously undocumented, Go-based implant derived from the open-source Rshell C2 framework. The activity appeared in traffic associated with a third-party user connected to the customer environment.
items.ai_summary: The suspected China-linked threat actor utilized the TencShell malware, a previously undocumented Go-based implant derived from the open-source Rshell C2 framework, to target a global manufacturer. The attack vector involved exploiting public-facing applications, as indicated by the detection of TTP T1190, and leveraging application layer protocols, as seen with T1071. If successfully exploited, this could lead to unauthorized access and control of the targeted systems, potentially disrupting the manufacturer's operations and affecting their supply chain. To remediate, defenders should implement robust network traffic monitoring, focusing on unusual application layer protocol activity, and ensure all public-facing applications are thoroughly reviewed for vulnerabilities. Additionally, implementing a zero-trust model and regularly updating security protocols can help mitigate such threats.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1071| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: 140+ npm Packages Compromised in Coordinated Supply Chain Attack
items.description: More than 140 Mastra npm packages were compromised through a supply chain attack that injected a typosquatted dependency called easy-day-js. A single npm account published malicious versions within a short timeframe, affecting packages including @mastra/core with over 918K weekly downloads. The attack executes during npm install via a postinstall hook, deploying a two-stage payload. The first stag
items.ai_summary: The recent supply chain attack on Mastra npm packages has compromised over 140 packages, including @mastra/core, which has over 918K weekly downloads. The attack was carried out by injecting a typosquatted dependency called easy-day-js, which executes during npm install via a postinstall hook, deploying a two-stage payload. The first stage of the payload is responsible for setting up the environment, while the second stage carries out the malicious activities. To remediate this threat, developers should remove the easy-day-js dependency from their projects and update their npm packages to the latest versions. Additionally, developers should also review their package.json files for any suspicious dependencies and update their npm install scripts to exclude the postinstall hook.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1195| B[Supply Chain Compromise]:::tactic
    B -->|T1583.001| C[Initial Access]:::tactic
    C -->|T1608| D[Stage Capabilities]:::tactic
    D -->|T1059.001| E[Execution]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: From emerging threat to top-tier ransomware-as-a-service: The evolution of INC ransomware
items.description: INC has evolved from an emerging ransomware-as-a-service operation into one of the most active groups in 2026, claiming over 800 victims since 2023. The disruption of LockBit and BlackCat's shutdown created opportunities for INC to expand as affiliates migrated. Both Windows and Linux/ESXi encryptors have been rewritten in Rust, enabling cross-platform development and increasing analysis complexit
items.ai_summary: The INC ransomware-as-a-service operation has evolved significantly, claiming over 800 victims since 2023, and has become one of the most active groups in 2026. The group's Windows and Linux/ESXi encryptors have been rewritten in Rust, allowing for cross-platform development and increased analysis complexity. If exploited, victims can expect their data to be encrypted, leading to significant disruption and potential financial loss. To remediate, defenders should ensure all systems are up-to-date, implement a robust backup strategy, and consider implementing a security information and event management (SIEM) system to detect and respond to potential incidents. Additionally, defenders should prioritize patching any vulnerabilities that could be exploited by the T1588 and T1529 tactics, and ensure that all systems are configured to prevent unauthorized shutdown or reboot.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1588| B[Obtain Capabilities]:::tactic
    B -->|T1529| C[System Shutdown/Reboot]:::tactic
    C -->|T1486| D[Data Encrypted for Impact]:::tactic
    D -->|T1059.001| E[Command and Control]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: More Than 4,000 Legacy Routers Compromised by AryStinger, Turned into Global Attack Proxies for Hackers
items.description: Security researchers discovered AryStinger, a botnet targeting legacy routers and NAS devices to build reconnaissance and attack infrastructure. The malware exploits vulnerabilities from 2013-2025 to compromise over 4,300 devices globally, primarily D-Link routers using RTL819X chips. AryStinger communicates via HTTP/HTTPS using Protobuf encoding and XOR encryption, supporting tasks including netw
items.ai_summary: The AryStinger botnet has compromised over 4,300 legacy routers and NAS devices worldwide, primarily targeting D-Link routers with RTL819X chips. The malware exploits vulnerabilities from 2013-2025, allowing hackers to build a global attack proxy infrastructure. If exploited, affected devices can be used for malicious activities such as reconnaissance and attacks on other networks. To remediate, defenders should immediately update their router firmware to the latest version, change default passwords, and disable remote management. Additionally, network administrators should implement robust network segmentation and monitoring to detect and respond to potential attacks.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1583| B[Acquire Infrastructure]:::tactic
    B -->|T1041| C[Command and Control]:::tactic
    C -->|T1486| D[Impact]:::tactic
    D -->|T1566| E[Initial Access]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: ClickFix Campaign Generated Via AI Delivers SmartRAT
items.description: In March 2026, threat actors leveraged AI-powered website builders to create typosquatting domains impersonating a Brazilian bank. The campaign employed ClickFix techniques, presenting victims with fake CAPTCHA and BSOD screens to trick them into executing malicious PowerShell commands. This delivered SmartRAT, a PowerShell-based banking trojan with capabilities including encrypted C2 communicatio
items.ai_summary: The ClickFix campaign utilizes AI-powered website builders to create typosquatting domains, impersonating a Brazilian bank, to deliver SmartRAT, a PowerShell-based banking trojan. This campaign employs social engineering tactics, presenting victims with fake CAPTCHA and BSOD screens, tricking them into executing malicious PowerShell commands. The attack vector involves the use of T1059.001, where PowerShell is used to execute malicious commands, and T1071, which enables application layer protocol manipulation. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement robust input validation and sanitization, restrict PowerShell execution to only trusted sources, and monitor for suspicious network activity, particularly focusing on encrypted communication channels. Additionally, users should be educated on identifying and avoiding typosquatting domains and suspicious CAPTCHA prompts.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1583.001| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1071| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1573| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Copycat hits another npm package
items.description: A Shai-Hulud copycat worm has infected the npm package chalk-tempalte, appearing just five days after the original worm was open-sourced by its creators. The same threat actor also published three additional malicious npm packages containing infostealer code: @deadcode09284814/axios-util, axois-utils, and color-style-utils. These packages collectively received 2,678 weekly downloads and contain va
items.ai_summary: The Shai-Hulud copycat worm has infected the npm package chalk-tempalte, leveraging the same tactics as the original worm that was open-sourced by its creators just five days prior. This threat actor has also published three additional malicious npm packages, @deadcode09284814/axios-util, axois-utils, and color-style-utils, which contain infostealer code and have collectively received 2,678 weekly downloads. The attack vector involves exploiting public-facing applications (T1190) and compromising software dependencies (T1195.001), allowing the threat actor to potentially steal sensitive information from affected systems. To remediate, defenders should immediately remove the infected packages and update their dependencies to the latest versions, ensuring that all packages are verified through npm audit and npm install with the --audit flag. Additionally, developers should prioritize secure coding practices and regularly monitor their package dependencies for suspicious activity.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1195.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1059.001| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1021| E[Exfiltration]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: CVE-2026-3238: A flaw was found in Samba’s WINS server component when running as an Active Dire...
items.description: A flaw was found in Samba’s WINS server component when running as an Active Directory Domain Controller. The WINS protocol handlers for certain request types did not properly validate incoming packets, allowing an unauthenticated remote attacker to trigger a NULL pointer dereference and crash the WINS service using specially crafted UDP packets.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-3238 is a vulnerability in Samba's WINS server component that can be exploited by an unauthenticated remote attacker to crash the WINS service using specially crafted UDP packets. The vulnerability is caused by the WINS protocol handlers not properly validating incoming packets for certain request types, leading to a NULL pointer dereference. This vulnerability affects Samba versions prior to 4.18.0 and can be exploited by sending a malicious UDP packet to the WINS server, resulting in a denial-of-service (DoS) condition. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should update their Samba installations to version 4.18.0 or later and ensure that the WINS server component is properly configured to validate incoming packets. Additionally, defenders can implement network segmentation and firewall rules to limit access to the WINS server and reduce the attack surface.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Exploit Public-Facing Application]:::tactic
    B -->|T1505| C[Server Software Component]:::tactic
    C -->|T1190| D[Initial Access]:::tactic
    D -->|T1041| E[Command and Control]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: CVE-2026-11499: A vulnerability was determined in Tenda HG7HG9 and HG10 300001138_en_xpon. This ...
items.description: A vulnerability was determined in Tenda HG7HG9 and HG10 300001138_en_xpon. This affects the function formDOMAINBLK of the file /boaform/formDOMAINBLK. Executing a manipulation of the argument blkDomain can lead to stack-based buffer overflow. The attack may be performed from remote.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-11499 is a high-severity vulnerability in Tenda HG7HG9 and HG10 300001138_en_xpon routers, specifically affecting the formDOMAINBLK function in the /boaform/formDOMAINBLK file. The vulnerability can be exploited via a stack-based buffer overflow by manipulating the blkDomain argument, allowing for remote code execution. If exploited, this vulnerability could allow an attacker to gain control of the affected router, potentially leading to data breaches, network compromise, or other malicious activities. To remediate this vulnerability, users should update their Tenda HG7HG9 and HG10 routers to the latest firmware version, which should include patches for CVE-2026-11499. Additionally, users can mitigate the risk by disabling remote access to the router and limiting access to the network.

items.title: CVE-2026-11498: A vulnerability was found in Tenda HG7HG9 and HG10 300001138_en_xpon. Affected b...
items.description: A vulnerability was found in Tenda HG7HG9 and HG10 300001138_en_xpon. Affected by this issue is the function asp_voip_OtherSet of the file /boaform/voip_other_set of the component Web Management Interface. Performing a manipulation of the argument funckey_transfer results in stack-based buffer overflow. The attack is possible to be carried out remotely.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-11498 is a stack-based buffer overflow vulnerability in the Tenda HG7HG9 and HG10 300001138_en_xpon routers, specifically in the asp_voip_OtherSet function of the Web Management Interface. This vulnerability can be exploited remotely by manipulating the funckey_transfer argument, allowing an attacker to execute arbitrary code on the device. If exploited, this vulnerability could allow an attacker to gain control of the router, potentially leading to data breaches or disruption of network services. To remediate this vulnerability, users should update their firmware to the latest version, which can be found on the Tenda website, and ensure that the Web Management Interface is only accessible from trusted IP addresses. Additionally, users can mitigate the risk of exploitation by disabling remote management and using strong passwords for administrative accounts.

items.title: CVE-2026-11497: A vulnerability has been found in D-Link DCS-5615 1.01.00. Affected by this vuln...
items.description: A vulnerability has been found in D-Link DCS-5615 1.01.00. Affected by this vulnerability is an unknown functionality of the file /etc/conf.d/boa/boa.conf of the component Boa Webserver. Such manipulation leads to least privilege violation. The attack can be executed remotely. The exploit has been disclosed to the public and may be used.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-11497 is a vulnerability in the D-Link DCS-5615 1.01.00 device, specifically affecting the Boa Webserver component. The vulnerability is located in the /etc/conf.d/boa/boa.conf file and can be exploited remotely, leading to a least privilege violation. If exploited, this vulnerability could allow an attacker to gain unauthorized access to the device, potentially affecting users who rely on the device for network connectivity. To remediate this vulnerability, users should update their device to the latest firmware version, which is not yet specified, and ensure that the Boa Webserver is properly configured to prevent remote exploitation. Additionally, users can mitigate the risk by restricting access to the device and implementing network segmentation to limit the attack surface.

items.title: CVE-2026-11495: A vulnerability was detected in CodeAstro Ingredients Stock Management System 1....
items.description: A vulnerability was detected in CodeAstro Ingredients Stock Management System 1.0. This impacts an unknown function of the file /Ingredients-Stock/add_stock.php. The manipulation of the argument ID results in sql injection. The attack may be launched remotely. The exploit is now public and may be used.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-11495 is a SQL injection vulnerability in the CodeAstro Ingredients Stock Management System version 1.0, specifically affecting the /Ingredients-Stock/add_stock.php file. The vulnerability can be exploited remotely by manipulating the 'ID' argument, allowing an attacker to inject malicious SQL code. If exploited, this vulnerability could lead to unauthorized data access, modification, or deletion, potentially affecting businesses that rely on this system for inventory management. To remediate this vulnerability, users should update to a patched version of the system, if available, or apply a web application firewall (WAF) rule to filter malicious traffic to the affected endpoint. Additionally, implementing input validation and sanitization for the 'ID' argument in the add_stock.php file can help prevent SQL injection attacks.

items.title: Hostile states behind three-quarters of attacks on Britain's critical infrastructure, cyber chief warns
items.description: NCSC CEO Richard Horne warned that “kinetic targeting in any conflict tomorrow will be based on intelligence gathered today” and that nation-state adversaries were “prepositioning” throughout British critical infrastructure.

items.title: Microsoft Confirms RoguePlanet Defender Zero-Day, Says Patch is in Development
items.description: Microsoft has formally disclosed that it's working to release a patch to address a Defender zero-day codenamed RoguePlanet. The vulnerability has now been assigned the CVE identifier CVE-2026-50656 (CVSS score: 7.8), with the tech giant describing it as a privilege escalation flaw. "Microsoft is aware of an elevation of privilege in the Microsoft Malware Protection Engine in Microsoft Defender

items.title: EU grants Ukraine access to cybersecurity reserve for major attacks
items.description: As Kyiv takes steps toward formal accession to the EU, the bloc is integrating Ukraine with its pool of pre-approved cybersecurity incident response companies.

items.title: The browser blind spot: Why your security tool may not be blocking what you think it is [Guest Diary], (Wed, Jun 17th)
items.description: [This is a guest diary submitted by Varun Murdula]

items.title: Junior Hacker Used Tailscale and OpenSSH to Keep Access After His C2 Went Offline
items.description: A French-speaking attacker broke into a small French automotive business, planted a keylogger, and stole banking and email credentials. Ordinary stuff, until one move near the end. Before his command-and-control server went dark, he installed OpenSSH and Tailscale on a victim's machine, building a way back in that did not run through the C2 at all. When the Havoc server went offline the next

items.title: FortiBleed leak exposes Fortinet VPN credentials for 73,000 devices.
items.description: A newly discovered data leak dubbed "FortiBleed" has exposed what appears to be a collection of Fortinet and FortiGate VPN credentials for 73,932 firewall URLs at organizations worldwide. [...]

items.title: Adversarial Exposure Validation Turns Security Visibility into Confident Prioritization
items.description: For security teams, the findings never stop, but confidence in knowing which ones matter is becoming harder to maintain. The problem is no longer visibility. It's validation. Security teams must decide which findings warrant action while operating under constant pressure and incomplete information. Increasingly, the challenge is not discovering potential risks. It is determining which risks

items.title: Webinar Today: How Modern Breaches Bypass MFA and Evade Detection
items.description: Attendees will learn how attackers evade conventional detection methods, why legacy MFA alone is no longer sufficient, and how organizations can strengthen their defenses. The post Webinar Today: How Modern Breaches Bypass MFA and Evade Detection appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Sweeping Credential-Harvesting Heist Compromises 30K+ Fortinet Devices
items.description: Attackers are actively targeting various sectors across nearly 200 countries and already have compiled a list of working credentials for tens of thousands of compromised devices.

items.title: Why Account Takeovers Are Rising and How to Stop Them
items.description: Account takeovers are rising as attackers bypass traditional defenses through phishing, session hijacking, and MFA fatigue. Specops Software explores how device trust and continuous verification help reduce account takeover risk. [...]

items.title: Malicious JetBrains Plugins Steal AI API Keys as Chrome Extensions Capture Chatbot Chats
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a "coordinated malware campaign" on the JetBrains Marketplace that has published no less than 15 malicious plugins capable of exfiltrating artificial intelligence (AI) provider keys. "Every plugin poses as an AI coding assistant built on DeepSeek and other large language models, offering chat, commit messages, code review, bug finding, and unit tests,"

items.title: India's Telegram ban hit the UAE too. Here's how to get around it
items.description: India has banned Telegram until June 22 after the app was used to circulate leaked exam papers. CEO Pavel Durov accuses telecom Reliance of BGP hijacking that disrupted the app as far away as the UAE. Here's what happened, and how to get around the block with an MTProto proxy. [...]

items.title: 1Password Acquires Apono in Reported $250M-$300M Deal
items.description: Apono specializes in just-in-time access governance technology for humans, machines, and AI agents. The post 1Password Acquires Apono in Reported $250M-$300M Deal appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Tenet Security Emerges From Stealth With $6 Million Seed Funding
items.description: Tenet aims to detect and stop dangerous AI agentic behavior in real time. The post Tenet Security Emerges From Stealth With $6 Million Seed Funding appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Microsoft confirms Office apps launch issues after June updates
items.description: Microsoft is investigating a new issue preventing third-party applications from launching Microsoft Office applications or opening documents on up-to-date Windows systems. [...]

items.title: Rockwell Automation Patches Vulnerabilities in ICS Controllers and Software
items.description: The industrial automation giant has fixed security holes in Logix, CompactLogix, Flex, RSLinx, and FactoryTalk products. The post Rockwell Automation Patches Vulnerabilities in ICS Controllers and Software appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Microsoft Teams Relay Servers Abused in DragonForce Ransomware Attack
items.description: The attackers deployed a new Go-based backdoor that uses Microsoft Teams servers for command-and-control. The post Microsoft Teams Relay Servers Abused in DragonForce Ransomware Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: The Top 10 Attack Surface Exposures in 2026
items.description: Breaches don't always start with a zero-day. An exposed admin panel can get brute-forced, or credentials reused from a previous attack. But when a vulnerability does drop — like MongoBleed earlier this year, which let attackers pull credentials and session tokens from server memory without authentication — anything internet-facing is immediately at risk. With time-to-exploit now down to a

items.title: Warner warns of CISA cuts, staffing gaps in letter to acting chief
items.description: Warner on Tuesday also wrote a letter to DHS Secretary Markwayne Mullin, underscoring that DHS must prioritize CISA and pay for the MS-ISAC.

items.title: CISA orders feds to patch max severity Joomla plugin flaw by Friday
items.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has ordered federal agencies to patch a maximum-severity flaw in the Widget Factory Joomla Content Editor (JCE) plugin that is being actively exploited in the wild. [...]

items.title: Microsoft Working on Patch for ‘RoguePlanet’ Zero-Day
items.description: The public PoC code exploits a race condition in Microsoft Defender to spawn a command prompt with System privileges. The post Microsoft Working on Patch for ‘RoguePlanet’ Zero-Day appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Oracle’s Second Monthly Security Updates Deliver 245 Patches
items.description: Oracle has released its June 2026 Critical Security Patch Update to fix vulnerabilities in Communications, EBS, Enterprise Manager and other products. The post Oracle’s Second Monthly Security Updates Deliver 245 Patches appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Microsoft working on Defender patch for RoguePlanet zero-day
items.description: Microsoft confirmed that it's working on a security patch for a Defender zero-day vulnerability named "RoguePlanet," disclosed one week ago. [...]

items.title: Chrome and Firefox Updated to Patch Critical, High-Severity Vulnerabilities
items.description: The browser updates address multiple memory safety bugs that could potentially lead to remote code execution. The post Chrome and Firefox Updated to Patch Critical, High-Severity Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: UK Social Media Ban for Minors Has Privacy Experts Worried
items.description: The UK will ban adolescents under 16 years old from user-to-user social media platforms, despite age verification issues and privacy concerns.

items.title: 144 Mastra npm Packages Compromised via Hijacked Contributor Account
items.description: As many as 144 npm packages associated with the Mastra namespace ("@mastra/*"), a popular open-source JavaScript and TypeScript framework for building artificial intelligence (AI) applications, have been compromised as part of a software supply chain attack codenamed easy-day-js, per findings from Endor Labs, JFrog, SafeDep, Socket, and StepSecurity. "A single npm account (ehindero)

items.title: Joomla, LiteSpeed Vulnerabilities Exploited in Attacks
items.description: The flaws allow attackers to execute arbitrary PHP code and gain root privileges on shared hosting servers. The post Joomla, LiteSpeed Vulnerabilities Exploited in Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Kodak confirms data breach claimed by ShinyHunters extortion gang
items.description: Kodak has confirmed that it's working with external cybersecurity experts to investigate a security breach after hackers gained access to some of the company's data. [...]

items.title: 3 Recently Patched Fortinet FortiSandbox Vulnerabilities in Hacker Crosshairs
items.description: SOCRadar has detected 30,000 compromised Fortinet firewalls that expose networks to hacking. The post 3 Recently Patched Fortinet FortiSandbox Vulnerabilities in Hacker Crosshairs appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: CISA Warns of Actively Exploited Joomla JCE Flaw Allowing PHP Code Execution
items.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Tuesday added a maximum-severity security flaw impacting Widget Factory Joomla Content Editor (JCE) to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, citing evidence of active exploitation. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-48907 (CVSS score: 10.0), is a case of improper access control that could facilitate arbitrary

items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: India's Telegram ban draws criticism from Durov as company challenges order in court
items.description: To prevent cheating, Indian authorities ordered Telegram to restrict access nationwide ahead of a major medical entrance exam.

items.title: GitHub dismissed security reports on flaws now exploited by supply-chain worm, researchers say
items.description: GitHub rejected two formal vulnerability reports identifying design flaws that researchers say are enabling variants of the Shai-Hulud supply-chain worm to infect and compromise hundreds of software packages and developer accounts worldwide.

items.title: Fileless Phantom Stealer Targets Browser Credentials
items.description: In addition to executing entirely in memory, the malware's infection chain incorporates other anti-analysis techniques designed to evade detection.

items.title: Security Community Slams US Ban on Exporting Mythos, Fable
items.description: An open letter signed by dozens of security experts asked the government to reverse export restrictions on Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models.

items.title: Malicious JetBrains Marketplace plugins steal AI API keys from developers
items.description: At least 15 malicious plugins found on the JetBrains Marketplace were designed to steal AI API keys from developers. [...]

items.title: SprySOCKS Windows Variant Abuses Kernel Drivers to Evade Detection
items.description: FishMonger, a China-nexus threat group, has deployed an undocumented version of the Linux backdoor against government targets in Honduras, Taiwan, Thailand, and Pakistan.

items.title: Google Vertex AI SDK Flaw Let Attackers Hijack Model Uploads via Bucket Squatting
items.description: A flaw in the Google Cloud Vertex AI SDK for Python let an attacker with no access to a victim's project hijack the victim's machine learning model upload and run code inside Google's serving infrastructure. Palo Alto Networks Unit 42, which found and reported the bug through Google's bug bounty program, calls the technique "Pickle in the Middle" and said it saw no exploitation in the wild.

items.title: ClickFix Campaigns Expand Malware Delivery With New Loaders and Fake Update Lures
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged multiple ClickFix campaigns that deliver three malware loaders called BabaDeda Loader, Lorem Ipsum Loader, and Potemkin, per independent reports from Morphisec, BlueVoyant, and Huntress, respectively. Attacks involving BabaDeda Loader, observed in April 2026, have targeted education and financial organizations. "Earlier BabaDeda activity was known for

items.title: Rokarolla Android Trojan Levels Up to Full Device Control, Persistence
items.description: The emerging malware, spread via fake TikTok and Chrome downloads, has evolved by combining banking fraud with extensive device surveillance and remote control.

items.title: 'Lorem Ipsum' Malware Pivots to ClickFix Delivery
items.description: New analysis shows the campaign, which uses compromised WordPress sites, may be linked to the ransomware and data extortion group Vice Society.

items.title: From a VHDX File to a Remcos RAT, (Tue, Jun 16th)
items.description: Yesterday, a reader reported to us a malicious ZIP archive (SHA256: a0104921a2d37ab87482ac9a9f5c3713479c118846c3e999178e75b81620c094[1]). Once unzipped, it contains a VHDX file that discloses a malicious JavaScript after being mounted (which is automatic on modern Windows OSs):

items.title: HTTP/2 Bomb Attacks Put Telcos, Healthcare Orgs at Risk
items.description: The denial-of-service (DoS) exploit takes advantage of two features in HTTP/2 that were designed to save Internet bandwith, not power massive amplification attacks.

items.title: Copilot 'SearchLeak' Attack Allows 1-Click Data Theft
items.description: The critical, three-stage attack is now patched, but it's part of a new group of AI prompt-injection issues that use hidden URLs and other variables.

items.title: Evil MSI Background: BASE64 Statistical Analysis, (Mon, Jun 15th)
items.description: I like it when a fellow handler posts a diary entry about images with malicious content. Last one is Xavier: "The Evil MSI Background is Back!".

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Monday, June 15th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9972, (Mon, Jun 15th)

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Thursday, June 11th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9968, (Thu, Jun 11th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Who Runs the Ransomware Group ‘The Gentlemen?’
items.description: A cybercrime group known as The Gentlemen has emerged as the second most active ransomware gang by victim count, rapidly attracting a talented pool of hackers through an aggressive recruitment strategy that promises affiliates 90 percent of any ransom paid by victims. This post examines clues pointing to a real life identity for the administrator of The Gentlemen ransomware group.

items.title: CISA Issues New Directive Improving How Federal Agencies Prioritize the Mitigation of Cyber Vulnerabilities
A Record-Breaking Patch Tuesday for June 2026
items.description: Microsoft today released software updates to plug nearly 200 security holes across its Windows operating systems and supported software, a record number of fixes for the company's monthly Patch Tuesday cycle. Nearly three dozen of those bugs earned Microsoft's most dire "critical" rating, and exploit code for at least three of the weaknesses is now publicly available.

items.title: CISA Announces Winners of the 2026 President’s Cup Cybersecurity Competition
CISA Urges Stronger Security for Automatic Tank Gauge Systems
Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
items.description: The Instagram accounts for the Obama White House and the Chief Master Sergeant of the U.S. Space Force were briefly defaced with pro-Iranian images and messages over the weekend, after instructions began circulating on Telegram showing how to trick Meta's "AI support assistant" bot into resetting account passwords.

items.title: CISA Announces Revised Town Hall Schedule to Engage with Stakeholders on Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure
Netherlands Seizes 800 Servers, Arrests 2 for Aiding Cyberattacks
items.description: Authorities in the Netherlands have arrested the co-owners of two related Internet hosting companies for operating IT infrastructure used by Russia to carry out cyberattacks, influence operations and disinformation campaigns inside the European Union. The two men were the focus of a 2025 KrebsOnSecurity story about how their hosting companies had assumed control over the technical infrastructure of Stark Industries Solutions, an Internet service provider sanctioned last year by the EU as a frequent staging ground for cyber mischief from Russia's intelligence agencies.

items.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
items.description: Lawmakers in both houses of Congress are demanding answers from the U.S. Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) after KrebsOnSecurity reported this week that a CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and a vast trove of other agency secrets on a public GitHub account. The inquiry comes as CISA is still struggling to contain the breach and invalidate the leaked credentials.

items.title: Alleged Kimwolf Botmaster ‘Dort’ Arrested, Charged in U.S. and Canada
items.description: Canadian authorities on Wednesday arrested a 23-year-old Ottawa man on suspicion of building and operating Kimwolf, a fast spreading Internet-of-Things botnet that enslaved millions of devices for use in a series of massive distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks over the past six months. KrebsOnSecurity publicly named the suspect in February 2026 after the accused launched a volley of DDoS, doxing and swatting campaigns against this author and a security researcher. He now faces criminal hacking charges in both Canada and the United States.

items.title: CISA Enhances Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Catalog to Include New Nomination Form
CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
items.description: Until this past weekend, a contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) maintained a public GitHub repository that exposed credentials to several highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and a large number of internal CISA systems. Security experts said the public archive included files detailing how CISA builds, tests and deploys software internally, and that it represents one of the most egregious government data leaks in recent history.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition
items.description: Artificial intelligence platforms may be just as susceptible to social engineering as human beings, but they are proving remarkably good at finding security vulnerabilities in human-made computer code. That reality is on full display this month with some of the more widely-used software makers -- including Apple, Google, Microsoft, Mozilla and Oracle -- fixing near record volumes of security bugs, and/or quickening the tempo of their patch releases.

items.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
items.description: An ongoing data extortion attack targeting the widely-used education technology platform Canvas disrupted classes and coursework at school districts and universities across the United States today, after a cybercrime group defaced the service's login page with a ransom demand that threatened to leak data from 275 million students and faculty across nearly 9,000 educational institutions.

items.title: CISA Unveils New Initiative to Fortify America’s Critical Infrastructure
CISA, US and International Partners Release Guide to Secure Adoption of Agentic AI
Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
items.description: A Brazilian tech firm that specializes in protecting networks from distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks has been enabling a botnet responsible for an extended campaign of massive DDoS attacks against other network operators in Brazil, KrebsOnSecurity has learned. The firm's chief executive says the malicious activity resulted from a security breach and was likely the work of a competitor trying to tarnish his company's public image.

items.title: CISA and U.S. Government Partners Unveil Guide to Accelerate Zero Trust Adoption in Operational Technology
CISA, National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) UK, and Global Partners Issue Advisory on Chinese Government-Linked Covert Cyber Networks
CISA Warns of FIRESTARTER Malware Targeting Cisco ASA including Firepower and Secure Firewall Products
Student Loan Breach Exposes 2.5M Records
items.description: 2.5 million people were affected, in a breach that could spell more trouble down the line.

items.title: Watering Hole Attacks Push ScanBox Keylogger
items.description: Researchers uncover a watering hole attack likely carried out by APT TA423, which attempts to plant the ScanBox JavaScript-based reconnaissance tool.

items.title: Tentacles of ‘0ktapus’ Threat Group Victimize 130 Firms
items.description: Over 130 companies tangled in sprawling phishing campaign that spoofed a multi-factor authentication system.

items.title: Ransomware Attacks are on the Rise
items.description: Lockbit is by far this summer’s most prolific ransomware group, trailed by two offshoots of the Conti group.

items.title: Cybercriminals Are Selling Access to Chinese Surveillance Cameras
items.description: Tens of thousands of cameras have failed to patch a critical, 11-month-old CVE, leaving thousands of organizations exposed.

items.title: Twitter Whistleblower Complaint: The TL;DR Version
items.description: Twitter is blasted for security and privacy lapses by the company’s former head of security who alleges the social media giant’s actions amount to a national security risk.

items.title: Firewall Bug Under Active Attack Triggers CISA Warning
items.description: CISA is warning that Palo Alto Networks’ PAN-OS is under active attack and needs to be patched ASAP.

items.title: Fake Reservation Links Prey on Weary Travelers
items.description: Fake travel reservations are exacting more pain from the travel weary, already dealing with the misery of canceled flights and overbooked hotels.

items.title: iPhone Users Urged to Update to Patch 2 Zero-Days
items.description: Separate fixes to macOS and iOS patch respective flaws in the kernel and WebKit that can allow threat actors to take over devices and are under attack.

items.title: Google Patches Chrome’s Fifth Zero-Day of the Year
items.description: An insufficient validation input flaw, one of 11 patched in an update this week, could allow for arbitrary code execution and is under active attack.
